was the 20th year in the history of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC), a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") promotion based in the United States. 2012 started with [UFC 142: Aldo vs. Mendes](/wiki/UFC_142 "UFC 142") and ended with [UFC 155: dos Santos vs. Velasquez 2](/wiki/UFC_155 "UFC 155"). The year saw the successful UFC Featherweight title defense by [José Aldo](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Aldo "José Aldo"), the crowning of [Carlos Condit](/wiki/Carlos_Condit "Carlos Condit") as the Interim Welterweight Champion, a new Lightweight Champion in [Benson Henderson](/wiki/Benson_Henderson "Benson Henderson"), a tournament to crown the first UFC Flyweight Champion as well as the finales of *[The Ultimate Fighter 15](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_15 "The Ultimate Fighter 15")* and *[The Ultimate Fighter: Brazil](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Brazil "The Ultimate Fighter: Brazil")*.

The year **2012** was the 20th year in the history of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC), a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") promotion based in the United States. 2012 started with [UFC 142: Aldo vs. Mendes](/wiki/UFC_142 "UFC 142") and ended with [UFC 155: dos Santos vs. Velasquez 2](/wiki/UFC_155 "UFC 155"). The year saw the successful UFC Featherweight title defense by [José Aldo](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Aldo "José Aldo"), the crowning of [Carlos Condit](/wiki/Carlos_Condit "Carlos Condit") as the Interim Welterweight Champion, a new Lightweight Champion in [Benson Henderson](/wiki/Benson_Henderson "Benson Henderson"), a tournament to crown the first UFC Flyweight Champion as well as the finales of *[The Ultimate Fighter 15](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_15 "The Ultimate Fighter 15")* and *[The Ultimate Fighter: Brazil](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Brazil "The Ultimate Fighter: Brazil")*.

***UFC on Fox: Evans vs. Davis*** (also known as ***UFC on Fox 2***) was a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") event held by the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") on January 28, 2012, at the [United Center](/wiki/United_Center "United Center") in [Chicago, Illinois](/wiki/Chicago,_Illinois "Chicago, Illinois").[[39]](#cite_note-UFCFOX2-52)

### Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2012_in_UFC&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Background")]

The event was the first to be broadcast on [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") as part of a seven-year agreement between the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") and the network and the second to air live on [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") after the inaugural *[UFC on Fox: Velasquez vs. Dos Santos](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox:_Velasquez_vs._Dos_Santos "UFC on Fox: Velasquez vs. Dos Santos")*.[[40]](#cite_note-FOX,_UFC_announce_multiyear_deal-53)

A bout between [Demetrious Johnson](/wiki/Demetrious_Johnson_(fighter) "Demetrious Johnson (fighter)") and [Eddie Wineland](/wiki/Eddie_Wineland "Eddie Wineland") was originally slated for this event. However, Johnson was pulled from the bout to be a participant in the UFC's [Flyweight](/wiki/Flyweight_(MMA) "Flyweight (MMA)") tournament set to begin in March. Wineland was then expected to face [Johnny Bedford](/wiki/Johnny_Bedford "Johnny Bedford") at this event.[[41]](#cite_note-54) However, Wineland himself was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by promotional newcomer [Mitch Gagnon](/wiki/Mitch_Gagnon "Mitch Gagnon").[[42]](#cite_note-55) Then just days before the event, the bout was cancelled due to an alleged visa issue for Gagnon.[[43]](#cite_note-56)

[Paul Sass](/wiki/Paul_Sass "Paul Sass") was expected to face [Evan Dunham](/wiki/Evan_Dunham "Evan Dunham"), but was forced from the bout with an injury and replaced by [Nik Lentz](/wiki/Nik_Lentz "Nik Lentz").[[44]](#cite_note-57)

[Cody McKenzie](/wiki/Cody_McKenzie "Cody McKenzie") was expected to face [Michael Johnson](/wiki/Michael_Johnson_(fighter) "Michael Johnson (fighter)") at the event, but was forced from the bout with an injury and replaced by [Shane Roller](/wiki/Shane_Roller "Shane Roller").[[45]](#cite_note-58)

[Mark Muñoz](/wiki/Mark_Mu%C3%B1oz "Mark Muñoz") was expected to face [Chael Sonnen](/wiki/Chael_Sonnen "Chael Sonnen") in a #1 contender's bout for a [middleweight](/wiki/Middleweight_(MMA) "Middleweight (MMA)") title shot, but Munoz was forced out of the bout with an injury. [Michael Bisping](/wiki/Michael_Bisping "Michael Bisping"), who was pulled from a scheduled fight with [Demian Maia](/wiki/Demian_Maia "Demian Maia"),[[46]](#cite_note-59) replaced Munoz to face Sonnen in the #1 contender's bout. [Chris Weidman](/wiki/Chris_Weidman "Chris Weidman") would step in on 11 days notice to face Maia as Bisping's replacement.[[47]](#cite_note-60)

The preliminary bout between [Chris Camozzi](/wiki/Chris_Camozzi "Chris Camozzi") and [Dustin Jacoby](/wiki/Dustin_Jacoby "Dustin Jacoby") was not aired on television or [Facebook](/wiki/Facebook "Facebook"), making it the first bout on a [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") card to go unaired on the event's live broadcast since [UFC Fight Night: Nogueira vs. Davis](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_Nogueira_vs._Davis "UFC Fight Night: Nogueira vs. Davis") in March 2011.[[48]](#cite_note-61)

The event averaged 4.7 million viewers with a ratings peak of 6 million viewers for the main event.[[49]](#cite_note-62)

***UFC on Fuel TV: Sanchez vs. Ellenberger*** (also known as ***UFC on Fuel TV 1***), was the first UFC event to air exclusively on [Fuel TV](/wiki/Fox_Sports_2#History "Fox Sports 2"). It was held on February 15, 2012, at the [Omaha Civic Auditorium](/wiki/Omaha_Civic_Auditorium "Omaha Civic Auditorium") in [Omaha, Nebraska](/wiki/Omaha,_Nebraska "Omaha, Nebraska").[[52]](#cite_note-UFC_on_Fuel_TV_1-67) At the time, the event was the most watched show ever aired on Fuel TV with 217,000 viewers tuning in.[[53]](#cite_note-68)

### Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2012_in_UFC&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Background")]

The main event consisted of [Diego Sanchez](/wiki/Diego_Sanchez "Diego Sanchez") facing [Jake Ellenberger](/wiki/Jake_Ellenberger "Jake Ellenberger"). Ellenberger came into the match on a five fight win streak, four of which ended by stoppage, including one against [Jake Shields](/wiki/Jake_Shields "Jake Shields"). The fight against Ellenberger would be Sanchez's seventeenth fight with the UFC going back to April 2005 when he won *[The Ultimate Fighter 1](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_1 "The Ultimate Fighter 1")*.[[54]](#cite_note-69) Ellenberger continued his win streak defeating Sanchez by unanimous decision.

[Rani Yahya](/wiki/Rani_Yahya "Rani Yahya") was expected to face [Jonathan Brookins](/wiki/Jonathan_Brookins "Jonathan Brookins") at the event. However, Yahya was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Vagner Rocha](/wiki/Vagner_Rocha "Vagner Rocha").[[55]](#cite_note-70)

Promotional newcomers Yoislandy Izquierdo and Bernardo Magalhaes were expected to face each other at the event. However, Izquierdo was not allowed to compete due to a contractual dispute with another organization and was replaced by [Tim Means](/wiki/Tim_Means "Tim Means").[[56]](#cite_note-71)

A bout between Buddy Roberts and Sean Loeffler was scheduled for the preliminary card. However, the pairing was scrapped on the day of the event as Loeffler injured his ankle during a pre-fight warm up.[[57]](#cite_note-72) With the loss of the Roberts/Loeffler bout, the event took place with only nine fights, making it the smallest fight card for the promotion since [UFC 110](/wiki/UFC_110 "UFC 110") in 2010.

***UFC on Fuel TV: The Korean Zombie vs. Poirier*** (also known as ***UFC on Fuel TV 3***) was a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") event held by the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") on May 15, 2012, at the [Patriot Center](/wiki/Patriot_Center "Patriot Center") in [Fairfax, Virginia](/wiki/Fairfax,_Virginia "Fairfax, Virginia").[[60]](#cite_note-UFCFuel3-76) Initially, this event was referred to as *UFC on FX 3*, but was since clarified to be a [Fuel TV](/wiki/Fox_Sports_2#History "Fox Sports 2") event.[[61]](#cite_note-Fuel_Not_FX-77)

### Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2012_in_UFC&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Background")]

[Brandon Vera](/wiki/Brandon_Vera "Brandon Vera") was briefly scheduled to face [Thiago Silva](/wiki/Thiago_Silva_(fighter) "Thiago Silva (fighter)") in a rematch of their January 2011 bout. However, Vera was forced out of the bout with an injury. [Igor Pokrajac](/wiki/Igor_Pokrajac "Igor Pokrajac") stepped in to fight Silva.[[62]](#cite_note-78) Silva was subsequently pulled from the bout to replace an injured [Antônio Rogério Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rog%C3%A9rio_Nogueira "Antônio Rogério Nogueira") against [Alexander Gustafsson](/wiki/Alexander_Gustafsson "Alexander Gustafsson") at *[UFC on Fuel TV: Gustafsson vs. Silva](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV:_Gustafsson_vs._Silva "UFC on Fuel TV: Gustafsson vs. Silva")*.[[63]](#cite_note-UFCSwedenchange-79) Pokrajac will now face [Fabio Maldonado](/wiki/Fabio_Maldonado "Fabio Maldonado").[[64]](#cite_note-Pokrajac/Maldonado-80)

[Yves Edwards](/wiki/Yves_Edwards "Yves Edwards") was expected to face [Donald Cerrone](/wiki/Donald_Cerrone "Donald Cerrone") at the event, but Edwards was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Jeremy Stephens](/wiki/Jeremy_Stephens "Jeremy Stephens").[[65]](#cite_note-StephensIn-81)

[Mike Easton](/wiki/Mike_Easton "Mike Easton") was expected to face [Yves Jabouin](/wiki/Yves_Jabouin "Yves Jabouin") at the event, but Easton was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Jeff Hougland](/wiki/Jeff_Hougland "Jeff Hougland") who was previously expected to face [Renan Barão](/wiki/Renan_Bar%C3%A3o "Renan Barão") at [UFC 148](/wiki/UFC_148 "UFC 148").[[66]](#cite_note-HouglandIn-82)

[Aaron Riley](/wiki/Aaron_Riley "Aaron Riley") was expected to face [Cody McKenzie](/wiki/Cody_McKenzie "Cody McKenzie") at the event, but Riley was pulled from the event and replaced by promotional newcomer [Marcus LeVesseur](/wiki/Marcus_LeVesseur "Marcus LeVesseur").[[67]](#cite_note-LeVesseurIn-83)

Azamat Gashimov was expected to make his promotional debut against Alex Soto. However, Gashimov was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by returning veteran [Francisco Rivera](/wiki/Francisco_Rivera_(fighter) "Francisco Rivera (fighter)").[[68]](#cite_note-RiveraIn-84)

The main event featured [Chan Sung Jung](/wiki/Chan_Sung_Jung "Chan Sung Jung") facing [Dustin Poirier](/wiki/Dustin_Poirier "Dustin Poirier"). The winner of this bout was linked to a potential bout with [José Aldo](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Aldo "José Aldo") for the UFC Featherweight Championship, but that fight did not materialize until 15 months later at [UFC 163](/wiki/UFC_163 "UFC 163").[[69]](#cite_note-85)

***UFC on FX: Johnson vs. McCall*** (also known as ***UFC on FX 3***) was a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") event held by the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") on June 8, 2012, at the [BankAtlantic Center](/wiki/BankAtlantic_Center "BankAtlantic Center") in [Sunrise, Florida](/wiki/Sunrise,_Florida "Sunrise, Florida").[[75]](#cite_note-91)

### Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2012_in_UFC&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Background")]

Because of a scoring error at [UFC on FX: Alves vs. Kampmann](/wiki/UFC_on_FX:_Alves_vs._Kampmann "UFC on FX: Alves vs. Kampmann"), a majority draw between [Demetrious Johnson](/wiki/Demetrious_Johnson "Demetrious Johnson") and [Ian McCall](/wiki/Ian_McCall_(fighter) "Ian McCall (fighter)") was mistakenly announced as a victory for Johnson.[[76]](#cite_note-92) [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White") announced the error following the conclusion of the event and that Johnson and McCall would have a rematch. The rematch was initially reported to happen at [The Ultimate Fighter 15 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Live#The_Ultimate_Fighter_15_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Live"), but instead was moved to the main card of this event, *UFC on FX 3*.[[77]](#cite_note-93) Since then, it was announced by the UFC that the fight would be the main event of *UFC on FX 3*.[[78]](#cite_note-94)

This was the first event that the UFC hosted in [South Florida](/wiki/South_Florida "South Florida") since [UFC Fight Night 10](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_10 "UFC Fight Night 10") in 2007. This also was the first UFC event to be headlined by a flyweight bout.

***UFC 149: Faber vs. Barão*** was a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") event held by the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") on July 21, 2012, at the [Scotiabank Saddledome](/wiki/Scotiabank_Saddledome "Scotiabank Saddledome") in [Calgary](/wiki/Calgary "Calgary"). It was the first event that the UFC has held in the [Canadian](/wiki/Canada "Canada") province of [Alberta](/wiki/Alberta "Alberta").[[83]](#cite_note-101)[[84]](#cite_note-102)

### Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2012_in_UFC&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Background")]

On April 24, due to a conflict with the UN Conference, the [UFC Middleweight Championship](/wiki/UFC_Middleweight_Championship "UFC Middleweight Championship") bout between [Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva") and [Chael Sonnen](/wiki/Chael_Sonnen "Chael Sonnen"), which was originally scheduled to main event [UFC 147](/wiki/UFC_147 "UFC 147") was moved to [UFC 148](/wiki/UFC_148 "UFC 148") to serve as the new main event. Plans were then being made for [José Aldo](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Aldo "José Aldo"), who at the time was scheduled to defend his title at this event against an undetermined opponent, to headline [UFC 147](/wiki/UFC_147 "UFC 147").[[85]](#cite_note-BelfortSilva-103) However it was reported on April 28, 2012, that Aldo would remain on this card and defend his title against [Erik Koch](/wiki/Erik_Koch "Erik Koch").[[86]](#cite_note-104)

The bout between [Michael Bisping](/wiki/Michael_Bisping "Michael Bisping") and [Tim Boetsch](/wiki/Tim_Boetsch "Tim Boetsch"), originally scheduled for [UFC 148](/wiki/UFC_148 "UFC 148"), was moved to this event to help bolster this (the UFC 149) fight card.[[87]](#cite_note-105) However, Bisping was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by promotional newcomer [Hector Lombard](/wiki/Hector_Lombard "Hector Lombard").[[88]](#cite_note-106)

[Yoshihiro Akiyama](/wiki/Yoshihiro_Akiyama "Yoshihiro Akiyama") was expected to face [Thiago Alves](/wiki/Thiago_Alves_(fighter) "Thiago Alves (fighter)") at this event, but was forced to pull out of the fight due to injury and replaced by [Siyar Bahadurzada](/wiki/Siyar_Bahadurzada "Siyar Bahadurzada").[[89]](#cite_note-107) Then on June 1, Alves pulled out of the bout citing an injury[[90]](#cite_note-108) and was replaced by [Chris Clements](/wiki/Chris_Clements_(fighter) "Chris Clements (fighter)").[[91]](#cite_note-109) However, Bahadurzada was also forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Matthew Riddle](/wiki/Matthew_Riddle "Matthew Riddle").[[92]](#cite_note-110)

[Thiago Silva](/wiki/Thiago_Silva_(fighter) "Thiago Silva (fighter)") was expected to face [Maurício Rua](/wiki/Maur%C3%ADcio_Rua "Maurício Rua") at the event. However, Silva was forced out of the bout with an injury and Rua was pulled from the event entirely, and would face [Brandon Vera](/wiki/Brandon_Vera "Brandon Vera") at [UFC on Fox 4](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_4 "UFC on Fox 4").[[93]](#cite_note-111)

On June 4, 2012, it was announced that the UFC signed former [DREAM](/wiki/DREAM_(mixed_martial_arts) "DREAM (mixed martial arts)") champion [Bibiano Fernandes](/wiki/Bibiano_Fernandes "Bibiano Fernandes"). He was expected to compete against [Roland Delorme](/wiki/Roland_Delorme "Roland Delorme").[[94]](#cite_note-112) However, a week later it was announced that Fernandes was removed from the event due to injury but was later announced that Fernandes had not signed with the UFC. [Francisco Rivera](/wiki/Francisco_Rivera_(fighter) "Francisco Rivera (fighter)") filled in to fight Delorme.[[95]](#cite_note-Roland/Rivera-113)

On June 9, 2012, it was announced that UFC featherweight champion, [José Aldo](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Aldo "José Aldo") was removed from the event due to injury. No replacement has yet been named for his scheduled opponent, [Erik Koch](/wiki/Erik_Koch "Erik Koch").[[96]](#cite_note-114) As a result, the UFC Interim Bantamweight Championship fight between [Urijah Faber](/wiki/Urijah_Faber "Urijah Faber") and [Renan Barão](/wiki/Renan_Bar%C3%A3o "Renan Barão") was rescheduled as the main event of the fight.[[97]](#cite_note-Faber/Barão-115)

[George Roop](/wiki/George_Roop "George Roop") was scheduled to fight [Antonio Carvalho](/wiki/Antonio_Carvalho "Antonio Carvalho"), but on June 26, Roop was forced out of the fight and replaced by [Daniel Pineda](/wiki/Daniel_Pineda_(fighter) "Daniel Pineda (fighter)").[[98]](#cite_note-116)

Former [Pride](/wiki/Pride "Pride") and Interim [UFC Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Heavyweight_Championship "UFC Heavyweight Championship") [Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rodrigo_Nogueira "Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira") was expected to face [Cheick Kongo](/wiki/Cheick_Kongo "Cheick Kongo") at the event. However, Nogueira pulled out of the bout, citing that an arm injury sustained in his last bout had not healed enough to resume the proper training and was replaced by [Shawn Jordan](/wiki/Shawn_Jordan "Shawn Jordan").[[99]](#cite_note-117)

[Claude Patrick](/wiki/Claude_Patrick "Claude Patrick") was expected to face [James Head](/wiki/James_Head_(fighter) "James Head (fighter)") at the event. However, Patrick was forced out of the bout with an injury and was replaced by [Brian Ebersole](/wiki/Brian_Ebersole "Brian Ebersole").[[100]](#cite_note-118)
